multiAgent.title: Multi-Agent
multiAgent.subtitle: Deploy multi-agent collaboration workflows for complex automation
multiAgent.author: Author
multiAgent.quickSetupConfirmDesc: This will add the following capabilities to the current agent:
multiAgent.deployModeEnhanceDesc: Add workflow capabilities to the current agent, enabling it to call subagents. Best for quick start and single-agent scenarios.
multiAgent.deployModeDeployDesc: Create multiple independent subagents that can be called by the main agent. Best for complex tasks requiring specialized roles.
multiAgent.nextStepsDesc: Subagents have been created and the main agent has been configured. Now you can:
multiAgent.nextStep1: Send tasks to the main agent, it will automatically call appropriate subagents
multiAgent.nextStep2: Use /subagents list command to check subagent running status
multiAgent.nextStep3: Use /subagents send <number> <message> to send messages to a specific subagent
multiAgent.nextStepsTip: 💡 Tip: The main agent has learned how to use the sessions_spawn tool to call subagents
multiAgent.collaborationTipDesc: Choose "Enhance Agent" to teach the main agent how to call subagents, or "Deploy Subagents" to create independent specialized agents. After deployment, the main agent will automatically use sessions_spawn to call appropriate subagents.
multiAgent.promptPlaceholder: Leave empty to use the built-in default prompt (recommended). Paste a custom prompt here to override.

workflow.taskPlaceholder: Describe the task for this workflow...
workflow.deployFirst: Please deploy subagents first using the "Deploy Subagents" button before running the workflow.

templateManager.title: Knowledge Center
templateManager.subtitle: Scenarios, agents, knowledge base and templates
templateManager.knowledgeHubDesc: Recipes, tips, snippets, and FAQs for mastering OpenClaw
templateManager.recipesDesc: Step-by-step configuration guides and best practices
templateManager.tipsDesc: Quick knowledge cards for power users
templateManager.noKnowledgeDesc: Knowledge content will be added here — recipes, tips, snippets, and FAQs
templateManager.applyAppendConfirm: This will append content to {file}. A backup will be created. Continue?
templateManager.applyReplaceConfirm: This will replace the contents of {file}. A backup will be created. Continue?

templateSection.title: Template Center
templateSection.subtitle: Browse and install templates
templateSection.deleteConfirm: Are you sure you want to delete this template?

templateSource.title: Template Sources
templateSource.subtitle: Manage template sources and cache
templateSource.confirmDelete: Are you sure you want to delete this source?
templateSource.sourceDescBuiltin: Pre-installed templates that work offline, no network required
templateSource.sourceDescCdn: Fast CDN delivery with auto-caching, updated regularly
templateSource.sourceDescGithub: Community templates from GitHub, always the latest version

multiAgentDeploy.title: Deploy Multi-Agent System
multiAgentDeploy.previewDesc: This will create multiple independent AI Agents, each with their own workspace and configuration.
multiAgentDeploy.prefixHint: Agents will be named: prefix-agentId (e.g., my-team-researcher)
multiAgentDeploy.coordinatorUpdatedDesc: SOUL.md has been updated with subagent orchestration info
multiAgentDeploy.coordinatorNotUpdatedDesc: Please manually configure the main agent

agentPicker.title: Select Agent
agentPicker.desc: Choose an agent to apply files to

scenarioTeamBuilder.cardDesc: Describe your scenario, AI auto-designs agents with SOUL.md & HEARTBEAT.md
scenarioTeamBuilder.title: AI Team Builder
scenarioTeamBuilder.subtitle: Describe a scenario, AI generates your team
scenarioTeamBuilder.descriptionPlaceholder: Describe what this team needs to do, their goals, and key responsibilities.
scenarioTeamBuilder.descriptionHint: Tip: Mention domain, tools, or communication style for better results
scenarioTeamBuilder.example1Desc: Full-stack team with PM, architects, frontend/backend devs and QA
scenarioTeamBuilder.example2Desc: Content creation team for articles, social media and SEO optimization
scenarioTeamBuilder.example3Desc: Multi-tier support with triage, specialists and escalation
scenarioTeamBuilder.generatingDesc: Designing agent roles, SOUL.md and HEARTBEAT.md files
scenarioTeamBuilder.modelRequiredTitle: Choose a generation model or configure a default model first
scenarioTeamBuilder.modelRequiredDesc: You are currently using "Auto (default model)", but OpenClaw does not have agents.defaults.model.primary configured yet. Please choose a generation model below, or configure a default model in OpenClaw and try again.
scenarioTeamBuilder.tpl_softdev_desc: Full-stack software development team with PM, architects, frontend/backend devs and QA. Covers requirement analysis, system design, coding, code review, testing, and delivery.
scenarioTeamBuilder.tpl_marketing_desc: Content creation and marketing team producing SEO articles, social media posts, email newsletters, and copy. Handles strategy, writing, editing, distribution, and analytics.
scenarioTeamBuilder.tpl_support_desc: Multi-tier customer support team handling inbound requests through triage, L1/L2/L3 specialists, and escalation. Focuses on fast resolution, knowledge base maintenance, and CSAT.
scenarioTeamBuilder.tpl_research_desc: Academic or market research team with lead researcher, domain experts, data analysts, and critical reviewers. Conducts literature review, data collection, analysis, and reporting.
scenarioTeamBuilder.tpl_ecommerce_desc: E-commerce team managing product listings, pricing, inventory, customer service, promotions, and analytics. Covers the full order-to-fulfillment workflow.
scenarioTeamBuilder.tpl_education_desc: Online education content team creating courses, quizzes, video scripts, and learning materials. Roles include instructional designer, subject expert, writer, and reviewer.
scenarioTeamBuilder.tpl_finance_desc: Financial analysis team covering market research, financial modeling, risk assessment, portfolio analysis, and investment reporting. Produces research reports and recommendations.
scenarioTeamBuilder.tpl_devops_desc: DevOps and SRE team managing CI/CD pipelines, infrastructure as code, monitoring, incident response, and deployment automation. Ensures reliability, security, and scalability.
scenarioTeamBuilder.wzStep1Done: Structure ready — click Next to continue
scenarioTeamBuilder.promptHint: You can freely edit this prompt before sending it to the AI.
scenarioTeamBuilder.genThinkingHint: This may take 1–10 minutes depending on team size and model.
scenarioTeamBuilder.directLlmDesc: Calls LLM directly — no tool execution, faster response
scenarioTeamBuilder.agentSessionDesc: Routes through OpenClaw agent — supports richer context
scenarioTeamBuilder.minimizeHint: You can minimize this window — generation continues in the background.
scenarioTeamBuilder.bgDoneToast: AI team generation complete — tap to view
scenarioTeamBuilder.timeoutTitle: AI is taking longer than expected
scenarioTeamBuilder.timeoutDesc: The AI may still be working. Try again — a shorter description or faster model may help.
scenarioTeamBuilder.timeoutTip: Tip: Try switching to a faster model
scenarioTeamBuilder.genErrGatewayDesc: The gateway connection was lost. Check gateway status and retry.
scenarioTeamBuilder.genErrGatewayTip: Check that the gateway is running and reconnect

multiAgentRoom.pauseMsg: This will immediately stop the agent turn in progress and switch the room to paused.
multiAgentRoom.deleteRoomMsgPrefix: Are you sure you want to delete room «
multiAgentRoom.deleteRoomMsgSuffix: »? This cannot be undone — all messages, members, tasks, and facts will be deleted.
multiAgentRoom.purgeSessionsMsg:
This will delete every member session on the upstream OpenClaw Gateway for this room (effective immediately).

The room’s messages, minutes, agenda, whiteboard, tasks, and other local data are all preserved and remain viewable.

Clean up?
multiAgentRoom.toastNextMeetingDraftPrefilled: Next-meeting draft prefilled from the retro suggestion — tweak it and start
multiAgentRoom.toastCloseoutSuccess: Closeout complete — check minutes, action items, and retro in the Outcome panel on the right
multiAgentRoom.toastCloseoutPartial: Closeout finished with some steps failing — check the result panel for what was generated
multiAgentRoom.toastMemberKickedSuffix: » has left — invite them back from the member list to restore
multiAgentRoom.toastMemberInvitedBackSuffix: » has been invited back and will speak again
multiAgentRoom.toastMemberUnmutedSuffix: » is unmuted and will speak again next turn
multiAgentRoom.toastMemberMutedSuffix: » is muted — speaking is skipped in this room (still in the meeting)
multiAgentRoom.removeMemberCascadeMessage: Also remove all messages sent by this member? Choose "Cancel" to keep their message history.
multiAgentRoom.toastMemberRemovedCascade: has been permanently deleted along with all their messages
multiAgentRoom.toastMemberRemovedKeep: has been permanently deleted — their messages are preserved
multiAgentRoom.toastMemberAdded: New member added — they will join the next round
multiAgentRoom.hintCloseout: Closeout is generating minutes, action items, playbooks, and retro
multiAgentRoom.hintClosed: Meeting is over — you can still view and clean up AI sessions
multiAgentRoom.hintPaused: Discussion paused — resume or adjust the next step
multiAgentRoom.hintDraft: Drop an opener or click Continue to officially start the session
multiAgentRoom.stateDraft: Room hasn’t fully warmed up yet
multiAgentRoom.stateBiddingDetail: All agents are scoring their intent to speak; the highest scorer will present next
multiAgentRoom.stateStartingDetail: Waiting for agents to pick up…
multiAgentRoom.stateIdleDetail: Click Continue or send a message to kick off the next round
multiAgentRoom.compactionWillRetry: · Will retry this turn with the compressed context
multiAgentRoom.budgetOver: Budget is about to exceed the cap
multiAgentRoom.helpSlashFork: — fork a new room from here
multiAgentRoom.busRemoveRuleMsg: This cross-room delivery rule will be removed. Continue?
multiAgentRoom.busSaveMsgActivePrefix: Saving will forward this room’s closeout output to the target room on future events. Active rules:
multiAgentRoom.busSaveMsgInactive: Saving will disable cross-room delivery for this room. Continue?
multiAgentRoom.rolesDeleteMsgSuffix: » will be deleted. Past member instances in rooms are preserved. Continue?
multiAgentRoom.settingsOverviewHint: Check the overall status first, then open the category you want to refine. Interactions stay consistent with the Room tuning wizard.
multiAgentRoom.settingsTuningDesc: Apply preset styles (casual / deep work / debate / brainstorm), or fine-tune speaking thresholds, memory compression, and persona prompts.
multiAgentRoom.auxCardSummary: Manage room-level Aux overrides and the workspace-wide default value.
multiAgentRoom.busCardSummary: Configure delivery rules, target rooms, and title templates for sending closeout results to other rooms.
multiAgentRoom.rolesCardSummary: Maintain reusable role templates — configure prompts, default model, Agent binding, and interaction preferences.
multiAgentRoom.auxDetailSubtitle: Manage the current-room Aux override and the global default
multiAgentRoom.auxIntroBody: is used for internal LLM calls in this room: competitive-speaking scoring, meeting minutes, TODO extraction, decision summaries, absence digests, etc. These calls are not quality-sensitive — a cheap model (gpt-4o-mini / gpt-5-mini / haiku) is recommended to save cost.
multiAgentRoom.auxIntroFooter: Main dialogue keeps using each member's own model and is not affected.
multiAgentRoom.auxStep1Hint: Highest priority. Only affects the current room. Leave empty to follow the next level.
multiAgentRoom.auxStep2Hint: Applied to rooms without their own override. Leave empty to fall back to members’ main models.
multiAgentRoom.auxStep3Hint: Fallback chain: room → global default → member main model. Below is the value actually used right now.
multiAgentRoom.auxEffectiveFallback: Follow members' main model (no cheap model configured)
multiAgentRoom.busDetailSubtitle: Configure rules for delivering closeout results to other rooms
multiAgentRoom.busIntroBody: Automatically forwards this room’s closeout output to another room as a new agenda item or task.
multiAgentRoom.busStep1Hint: Each rule describes one cross-room delivery: trigger event, target room, and agenda title template.
multiAgentRoom.busStep2Hint: These guardrails apply automatically — no extra configuration required. Just know what they do.
multiAgentRoom.busGuardrail1: Only rooms owned by the same user can deliver to each other; cross-owner is rejected.
multiAgentRoom.busGuardrail2: Direct A→B + B→A loops are avoided by default to prevent cascading triggers.
multiAgentRoom.busGuardrail3: Duplicate closeouts are deduplicated idempotently via a bundle marker — no duplicate agenda items.
multiAgentRoom.busStep3Hint: Once saved, new rules take effect on the next closeout.done event in the order shown above.
multiAgentRoom.busStep3HasChanges: You have unsaved changes. Save to apply.
multiAgentRoom.busStep3NoChanges: Rules are in sync with the server.
multiAgentRoom.busNoRulesHint: No delivery rules yet. Add a closeout → agenda automation.
multiAgentRoom.busTitleTemplatePlaceholder: Agenda title template, e.g. Retro source output: {{sourceRoomTitle}}
multiAgentRoom.busNotePlaceholder: Extra note (appended to the target agenda description)
multiAgentRoom.rolesDetailSubtitle: Maintain reusable role templates and behavior preferences
multiAgentRoom.rolesBrowseHint: Browse existing roles first. The editor on the right opens only after you pick a role or click "New role".
multiAgentRoom.rolesEmptyList: No roles in the library yet
multiAgentRoom.rolesPickFirstTitle: Pick a role to start editing
multiAgentRoom.rolesPickFirstHint: Or click "New role" above to create a new meeting-role template.
multiAgentRoom.rolesEditorDescEdit: Adjust metadata, prompt, and interaction preferences. Saving updates the template available for later rooms.
multiAgentRoom.rolesEditorDescNew: Create a reusable role template for quick role picking and batch reuse in meetings.
multiAgentRoom.rolesSectionBasicHint: Define the role’s card name, displayed identity, and sort order.
multiAgentRoom.rolesSectionPromptHint: Control the default model, role stance, Agent binding, and prompt style.
multiAgentRoom.rolesFieldDescriptionPlaceholder: Where this role fits and the angle it speaks from
multiAgentRoom.rolesFieldStylePromptPlaceholder: Style prompt (stylePrompt): constrains tone, style, and structure
multiAgentRoom.rolesSectionInteractionHint: Define which targets and topics the role prefers to collaborate with, challenge, or wrap up.
multiAgentRoom.rolesFieldClosureStylePlaceholder: closureStyle (e.g. summarize decisions / list action items)
multiAgentRoom.rolesFieldNotesPlaceholder: interaction notes: describe the role’s preferred interaction partners and collaboration / opposition style
multiAgentRoom.rolesFooterNote: Note: the role library is best for maintaining prompts, stance, agent binding, and scheduling preferences. Tool availability is still controlled by the underlying Agent / OpenClaw runtime — not restricted at the AI Meeting layer.
multiAgentRoom.heroDescLine1: AgentRoom is a native multi-agent meeting room. Put N AIs in one room,
multiAgentRoom.heroDescLine2: let them collaborate in real time, while you can jump in, rewrite replies, or play one of the roles yourself.
